rationale: Pinned review to resolved commit aec82bfde854d4ba24d137b942803eb6dc909a16. The round-3 fix reconciles the leading-dash guard with fixed CLI flags: buildCommand now owns the per-verb flag templates, while args are user-derived values only and are the only inputs subject to the leading-dash guard. Registration now calls buildCommand(CMD_PANEL_CREATE, slug, spec, target, bead_id?, round?) with no flag literals in args, and the re-panel note passes the round value N+1 while the template supplies --round. Step 6 remains coherent: its assertions pin the exact ALLOWED_CLI set, destructured identifier usage, absence/location of command literals, and single-builder routing; those constraints do not contradict the reconciled fixed-template model. I found no new contradiction or hole introduced by this delta.

findings.details: Fixed flags are now specified as buildCommand's fixed per-verb template, never as args, and the guard is scoped to user-derived values only.
